title: CosmosDB reminder partitioning not storing reminders in partitions
body:
When using CosmosDB as actor state store with reminder partitioning enabled, the reminders are not stored in the partitions anymore since runtime version 1.10.5 (v1.10.4 or lower is working correctly).
<!-- If you need to report a security issue please visit https://docs.dapr.io/operations/support/support-security-issues -->

## Expected Behavior

<!-- Briefly describe what you expect to happen -->
Actor reminders should be stored in partitions to optimize performance with CosmosDB.


## Actual Behavior

<!-- Briefly describe what is actually happening -->
The actor reminders are stored in a single CosmosDB document, causing high RU usage when many reminders are used.


## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

<!-- How can a maintainer reproduce this issue (be detailed) -->
I have created a sample project that demonstrates the bug:

https://github.com/arnoldpistorius/bug-dapr-cosmosdb-partitioning

Basically the steps are:

- Configure CosmosDB Container as actor state.
- In SDK enable actor reminder partitioning (eg. 3).
- Create a bunch of reminders through an actor.
- Observe the CosmosDB container's contents.
  - All reminders are stored in the same document
  - 2 different partitions were made, but both not used
  - After service restart additional partitions are made and some data is migrated, but still new reminders come in a single document.

When the same scenario is done using Redis as actor state store, the partitioning is working as expected.

## Release Note

<!-- How should the fix for this issue be communicated in our release notes? It can be populated later. -->
<!-- Keep it as a single line. Examples: -->

<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** New feature in Dapr. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Bug in runtime. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **UPDATE** Runtime dependency. -->

RELEASE NOTE:

This is still a valid issue..

I face same problem with dapr runtime 1.14.4, dotnet sdk 1.14.0

I use cosmosdb as actor state store. All reminders are saved in 1 document in cosmos db _by default_ with id convention: `actors||<actor type>`

I faced problem, that under high load (I have a lot of actors running at the same time, and each of them creates several reminders, update them and delete) I got too many 429 (The request rate is too large) and 412 errors (etag missmatch). 
So I decided to enable actor reminders partitioning based on this guide: https://docs.dapr.io/developing-applications/building-blocks/actors/howto-actors-partitioning/
Now I see many documents in actor state store with convention: `actors||<actor type>||<partition guid>||reminders||<N of partition>`. but all of them are empty, all reminders are still stored in `actors||<actor type>` document.
_____

Additional strange behaviour, which might be valid, but caught my attention is N of `actors||<actor type>||<partition guid>||reminders||<N of partition>` documents in db. I expected to see same number as I use in configuration when I register actor, which is 10 in my case
```csharp
options.RemindersStoragePartitions = 10
```
but instead, I see many groups of documents, each group has 10 documents and same partitionKey. It started with 2 groups, but during application work it created more and more groups, each has it's own partitionKey = partition guid and 10 documents (1 per partition) in it. All of them are empty

______
any plans to fix issue with partitioning in cosmos db soon? or maybe my understanding is wrong and it is not considered as a bug?

I'm seeing the same as @VenyaBrodetskiy. I'm running on Azure Container Apps, so they are still on version 1.13. Every few seconds (depending on traffic) it creates a complete new set of files. Cleaning them up is difficult, as they get the new partition keys each time.
